raw_consumer_statement:
XXXX XXXX  I made a reservation on XXXX for a house rental in XXXX ( XXXX ), XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. The listing advertised a full refund if canceled before XX/XX/XXXX. I paid the full amount of {$6100.00} to XXXX in XXXX XXXX on Citi XXXX credit card ( plus {$790.00} to XXXX for their fee ). XXXX reservation ID : XXXX, Property ID : XXXX XX/XX/XXXX - I canceled the reservation when we realized that the house was not going to work since we had a change in plans. I received an automated message from XXXX that the cancelation request was approved, and we would be refunded the amount of {$6100.00}. XXXX refunded their fee of {$790.00} on XX/XX/XXXX. XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX only refunded {$5800.00} on XX/XX/XXXX. 

XX/XX/XXXX - I first reached out to the host via XXXX messaging when we realized that we were only refunded {$5800.00} instead of the total amount {$6100.00}. The host ( XXXX XXXX XXXX said she was keeping the fee she was charged by the credit card company for the initial charge. XXXX itself expects her to refund the full amount I paid as documented in in an email. I reached out to XXXX on multiple occasions beginning on XX/XX/XXXX, with the last communication stating " your previous case has been escalated. '' ( XXXX case number XXXX. ) XXXX has never contacted me again. Since they already refunded me the amount they charged, they apparently have no obligation to enforce their own return/refund policy. 

XX/XX/XXXX - I filed a dispute with Citi credit card for {$6100.00} ( in error ). Re-filed and opened dispute on XX/XX/XXXX for the {$230.00} that we were not refunded. 

XX/XX/XXXX - Claim was denied despite submitting all documentation stating that the listing on XXXX says " full refund '' if canceled before XX/XX/XXXX. XXXX itself says that I should be getting the full amount of {$6100.00}. The host has taken it upon herself to make up her own rules about keeping the credit card fee which was not listed anywhere on the listing or on XXXXXXXX XXXX policies. Citi was wrong for denying our claim.
